en.extract:
The red myzomela (Myzomela cruentata) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.
It is found in New Guinea and New Britain. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
The reddish myzomela (M. erythrina) of New Ireland was formerly considered conspecific, but phylogenetic evidence indicates that both are distinct species, and it has thus been split from it by the IUCN Red List, BirdLife International, and the International Ornithologists' Union.
There are two recognized subspecies:

M. c. cruentata - New Guinea and Yapen
M. c. coccinea - New Britain and the Duke of York Islands


References


External links
Image at ADW

The '''red myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela cruentata''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].
It is found in [[New Guinea]] and [[New Britain]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Red Myzomela (Myzomela cruentata) - BirdLife species factsheet|url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/red-myzomela-myzomela-cruentata|access-date=2022-01-11|website=datazone.birdlife.org}}</ref>

The [[reddish myzomela]] (''M. erythrina'') of [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]] was formerly considered conspecific, but phylogenetic evidence indicates that both are distinct species, and it has thus been split from it by the [[IUCN Red List]], [[BirdLife International]], and the [[International Ornithologists' Union]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-06-13|language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite report|title=IOC World Bird List 11.2|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|language=en|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.11.2|doi-access=free}}</ref>

There are two recognized subspecies:<ref name=":1" />

* ''M. c. cruentata'' - New Guinea and [[Yapen]]
* ''M. c. coccinea'' - New Britain and the [[Duke of York Islands]]
